⚠️ **CRITICAL — WTW is BELOW its stop_loss_price.** Stop sits at 253.50; last observed price 247.72. The 2026-05-11 review (price 249.61) and 2026-05-14 review (247.72) both recorded `verdict='NONE'` — the stop logic appears to be **silently not firing** when price crosses the level (or `current_price` / `stop_loss_price` got out of sync after a trailing-stop ratchet from high-water-mark 259.91). Also: WTW was bought 2026-04-30 with `max_hold_days=14` — today is day **18, past max hold by 4 days**. Both issues need investigation tonight before market open.

CART is the only other position with tight stop headroom (+3.78%); the rest sit at ≥6.5%.

This is fine when the alternative is a -5.77% stop, but worth tracking: ~45% of closes go out via DESK_DECISION, and they're net slightly negative.

## 6. Broken-service / data-health flags

| Severity | Flag | Detail |
|---|---|---|
| **HIGH** | **WTW stop not firing** | Position 28: stop=253.50, current=247.72, last review 5/14 verdict=NONE. Stop was crossed by 2026-05-11 review (249.61). Either stop-trigger logic is broken or `current_price` is stale. Position is also past `max_hold_days` (18d held vs 14d limit). |
| **HIGH** | **`decision_tracking` table is empty** | 0 rows across 491 decisions for the full lifetime of the system. The post-decision price-walk tracker has never written a row. Backtesting and recommendation-accuracy work cannot proceed until this is wired up. |
| **HIGH** | **`ai_score` NULL on 481/491 (98.0%)** | Only 10 rows have `ai_score`, all on 2026-04-09. Field has been dead since day 1. 191 of those NULLs are on BUY/BUY_LIMIT/WATCH decisions where ranking matters. |
| **HIGH** | **`sector` NULL on 484/491 (98.6%)** | Last sector write was 2026-05-04. Sector clustering in this report had to be hand-inferred from ticker identity. |
| **HIGH** | **Stale `decision_points.status` for 40 CLOSED positions** | Every closed desk_position still carries `decision_points.status='Owned'`. The desk-exit code is not back-propagating status to `decision_points`. |
| **HIGH** | **`subscribers` table empty** | 0 rows — daily email summaries have nobody to send to. Either subscription onboarding is broken or there are simply no subs. |
| MEDIUM | **24 stuck PENDING decision_points >24h old** | Range 2026-04-09 → 2026-05-05 (APA, PBMRF, TPL, DOCN, NXT, ASTS, SNGNF, ASND, QXO/PB, FIG ×2, SSNC, BKRKY, CAR ×2, IBRX, FORM, IMO, CNA, FN, +4). Some look like data-fetch failures; QXO/PB has a slash in the ticker which suggests a parsing-path bug. |
| MEDIUM | **DR funnel gap: 43 BUY/BUY_LIMIT lifetime with no DR review** | Total BUY+BUY_LIMIT lifetime = 125; DR ran on only 82 of those (DR coverage 65.6% on names that should have been escalated). Daily distribution clusters around 2026-05-05 (4 gaps), 2026-05-09 (5 gaps), 2026-05-13 (2 gaps). Several gap days are weekends/holidays — but weekday gaps are real misses. |
| MEDIUM | **Status field uses two parallel vocabularies** | `decision_points.status` is {Owned, Not Owned, Pending}. `desk_positions.status` is {ACTIVE, CLOSED}. No automated link is keeping them consistent. Also: 4 synthetic rows on 2026-05-09 with symbols `T8_BUY`, `T8_BUY_LIMIT`, `T8_WATCH`, `T8_AVOID` (status='Pending') — looks like test fixtures left in prod DB. |
| MEDIUM | **MHVIY `decision_points.status` = 'Not Owned' but position is ACTIVE** | Row 449. Specifically broken status sync on the *entry* side (vs the slow stale-Owned issue above, which affects exits). |
| MEDIUM | **3 ERROR_PARSING DR cases (lifetime)** | HQY (4/10), PPERF (4/11), ADBE (4/23) — all fell through to AVOID. No recurrences since 4/23, but verify the parser still degrades safely. |
| MEDIUM | **PPERY mechanical-failure case** | DR-overridden 5/14 because the council read an 8% ex-dividend price adjustment as a real drop. The screener should mask ex-div drops before they reach the council; PM-level fix is downstream of the real bug. |
| MEDIUM | **Audit-report destinations unreachable** | Both primary write paths refused (see banner). Fifth consecutive run hitting this same failure. |
| LOW | **desk_reviews stale 4 days** | Last review timestamp 2026-05-14 19:36 ET. Reviews appear to have run daily through 5/14, then stopped. Today's run hasn't produced a review yet (only ~90 min into the session) — may be fine, but verify the desk-review scheduler hasn't drifted. |
| LOW | **`batch_comparisons` stale 10 days** | Last completed 2026-05-08. Lifetime only 10 batches. May be intentional (low-frequency feature) but flag if not. |

---

## 7. Insights & takeaways

- **The AI-infra cluster is selling off and the model is correctly skeptical.** 61% of today's haul is AI/semis/optical/data-center power; PM marked 7 of 11 as AVOID and the remaining 4 as WATCH. Recent DR overrides on AXON, CEG, IDCC, APP also flag AI/momentum exposure as the regime mismatch — DR is repeatedly citing "higher-for-longer rates" and "AI momentum deflation" as the reason to kill council BUYs. This is internally consistent.

- **DR is doing the heavy lifting.** Council survives only ~62% of the time when DR weighs in — the system's edge increasingly depends on DR being available and on-prompt. Verify the DR worker is healthy after today's session.

- **WTW is the canary for stop-loss reliability.** A position has held 4 days past max-hold *and* sat below its stop_loss_price across two consecutive reviews without triggering an exit. If this is real (not just a display sync issue), every active stop in the book is suspect. Highest-priority operational item.

- **Win rate 55% with avg +1.11% per closed trade gives roughly +44% lifetime sum-of-returns** — but this is equal-weighted unitless percentages, not capital-weighted P&L. The 15 TAKE_PROFIT wins (+6.28% avg) drive almost all of it; DESK_DECISION exits net flat-negative.

- **Data tail debt is the dominant systemic risk.** Three permanently-NULL columns (`ai_score`, `sector`, `decision_tracking`), 40 stale status rows, and 24 stuck PENDINGs are cumulative — none broke *today*, but together they cripple any retrospective evaluation and the planned backtester.

- **Subscribers = 0 means the email summary path is unused** — possibly de-prioritised on purpose, but the background email task at startup still runs against an empty list.

- **Today's screening was clean and on-baseline (18 vs 17.9 avg).** No reason to suspect a screener failure. The very low BUY/BUY_LIMIT rate (1 of 18 = 5.6%, vs lifetime 25.4%) is driven by the AI-infra concentration in the dip universe today, not a pipeline issue.

- **`T8_*` synthetic rows in `decision_points`** look like leftover test fixtures from 2026-05-09. They're harmless but pollute counts and would corrupt a backtest.

---

## 8. Suggested next actions

1. **(HIGH, today)** Pull WTW manually: confirm whether `current_price` is stale or the stop logic actually missed a fire. Either way, decide tonight whether to exit at market open. Then audit the trigger code path for every ACTIVE position.

2. **(HIGH, this week)** Wire `decision_tracking` writes back into the post-decision job. Without it, every "did the call work?" question is unanswerable and the backtester is dead.

3. **(HIGH, this week)** Backfill `decision_points.status` from `desk_positions.status` for the 40 closed rows and add a trigger / FK invariant so future closes propagate. Also fix the MHVIY entry-side mismatch (row 449).

4. **(HIGH)** Restore `ai_score` and `sector` population. Check whether the screener stopped writing them or whether the FastAPI startup task that backfills them is silently failing. Sector data is needed for clustering reports like this one.

5. **(MEDIUM)** Fix the audit-report destination problem so this file actually lands in the two intended folders — either bring them into the scheduled-task mount or change the task to point at `/Users/simonbaumgart/Antigravity/Stock-Tracker/audit_reports/` officially.

6. **(MEDIUM)** Force-resolve the 24 stuck PENDING rows: re-run them through the pipeline or mark them `PASS_INSUFFICIENT_DATA` so they stop showing up. Investigate the QXO/PB slash-ticker case.

7. **(MEDIUM)** Add an ex-dividend mask to the screener (PPERY-style mechanical drop should never reach the council). Tag each row with a `is_ex_div_adjustment` flag.

8. **(MEDIUM)** Delete the 4 `T8_*` synthetic rows from 2026-05-09 before they corrupt future analytics.

9. **(MEDIUM)** Confirm DR worker health: if ARGTF still has NULL `deep_research_review_verdict` in tomorrow's run, escalate.

10. **(LOW)** Decide whether to keep the email subscriber path. If yes, fix onboarding; if no, gate the background email task on subscriber count > 0.

11. **(LOW)** Restart `batch_comparisons` if intended to run daily (last completed 10 days ago).
